title: Daphnia magna: DNA extraction for downstream whole genome NGS
author: Albano Pinto

steps.title: Prepare the lysis solution
steps.instruction: Prepare the lysis solution: dilute 1 μl of Proteinase K (50 μg/μl) into 300 μl of Tissue and Cell Lysis Solution (both supplied by the kit) per sample.

steps.title: Add 150 μl of Tissue and...
steps.instruction: Add 150 μl of Tissue and Cell Lysis solution with proteinase K to each tube with the Sample and grind the daphnids using a pestle.

steps.title: Add more 150 μl of Tissue...
steps.instruction: Add more 150 μl of Tissue and Cell Lysis solution with proteinase K to each Sample tube and mix by inverting the tubes several times.

steps.title: Incubate the Sample tubes at 65°C...
steps.instruction: Incubate the Sample tubes at 65°C for 15 min in the thermoblock, and vortex the tubes every 5 minutes to mix.

steps.title: Cool the Sample down to 37°C
steps.instruction: Cool the Sample down to 37°C

steps.title: Incubate the Sample tubes at 50°C...
steps.instruction: Incubate the Sample tubes at 50°C for 3 hours in the thermoblock.

steps.title: Incubate the Sample tubes on ice for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate the Sample tubes on ice for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Add 150 μl of MPC protein...
steps.instruction: Add 150 μl of MPC protein precipitation solution (supplied by the kit) to each Sample tube. Mix by vortexing for 10 seconds.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4 °C for 10 minutes at 10000xg
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4 °C for 10 minutes at 10000xg.

steps.title: Discard the pellet (do not disturb...
steps.instruction: Discard the pellet (do not disturb the pellet) and transfer the supernatant to new sterile 1.5-mL eppendorf tubes.

steps.title: Add 500 μl of Isopropanol, 99
steps.instruction: Add 500 μl of Isopropanol, 99.5%, molecular biology grade Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #327272500 . Mix by gently inverting 40 times.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4 °C for 10 minutes at 13,000 rpm
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4 °C for 10 minutes at 13,000 rpm.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant (isopropanol) using a...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant (isopropanol) using a micropipette , carefully to not disturb the pellet. Centrifuge the tubes again for 10 seconds at room temperature and remove the remaining supernatant with a smaller bore pipette tip. Check if the DNA pellet (white) is visible.

steps.title: Add 500 μl of refrigerated Ethanol...
steps.instruction: Add 500 μl of refrigerated Ethanol 70% Contributed by users , gently , to avoid lifting the pellet. Spin-down the tubes at 4 °C if the pellet is loose. Discard the supernatant (ethanol) using a micropipette . Repeat this step 2 times.

steps.title: Dry the pelleted DNA at RT...
steps.instruction: Dry the pelleted DNA at RT for 30 minutes in the tubes with the lids open.

steps.title: Resuspend the DNA with 35-37 μl of autoclaved UP water
steps.instruction: Resuspend the DNA with 35-37 μl of autoclaved UP water. Leave the DNA at 4 °C for 30 minutes to aid in DNA suspending.

steps.title: Add 1 μl of 5 μg/μl...
steps.instruction: Add 1 μl of 5 μg/μl RNAse A (supplied by the kit) to each sample, then mix by inverting the tubes several times. Spin-down the samples.

steps.title: Run DNA quantification using fluorometry (recommended)
steps.instruction: Run DNA quantification using fluorometry (recommended)

steps.title: Prepare a 1-20 μL aliquot of...
steps.instruction: Prepare a 1-20 μL aliquot of sample (commonly 2 μL) and add the proprietary kit reagent for DNA quantification (dsDNA BR/HS) with Qubit to complete a final reading volume of 200 μL. For samples starting from higher biomass dilution might be needed before this step (check the expected DNA concentration vs. reading range).

steps.title: Run DNA quality screening via electrophoresis
steps.instruction: Run DNA quality screening via electrophoresis

steps.title: Run DNA quality screening using spectrophotometry
steps.instruction: Run DNA quality screening using spectrophotometry

steps.title: Read the solution in the Qubit for DNA quantification
steps.instruction: Read the solution in the Qubit for DNA quantification. The outcome will be the DNA concentration of the sample in ng/μL. If sample dilution applied, mind it to record the DNA concentration of the sample. For the reference samples as indicated in this protocol ( Sample ), the expected DNA concentration is generally within 5-10 ng/μL.

steps.title: Set the Nanodrop spectrophotometer software for nucleic acids assessment
steps.instruction: Set the Nanodrop spectrophotometer software for nucleic acids assessment

steps.title: Read the sample. The outcome will be
steps.instruction: Read the sample. The outcome will be: (i) Total nucleic acids concentration, typically within the range of 150-300 ng/μL if using samples similar to the reference sample in this protocol ( Sample ). As there is no consistent quantification of gDNA concentration only, this concentration is not particularly informative. (ii) Absorbance ratio 260/280 (~1.8 should be the target; appreciably lower ratios may indicate the presence of protein, phenol or other contaminants that absorb strongly at or near 280 nm). With this protocol, similar samples to that indicated as a reference ( Sample ) and using the indicated kit, ratios around 1.9 are commonly reached. (iii) Absorbance ratio 260/230 (2.0-2.2 should be the target; appreciably lower ratios indicate the presence of contaminants which absorb at 230 nm, such as EDTA, carbohydrates, phenol and some extraction reagents, e.g. TRIzol). With this protocol, similar samples to that indicated as a reference ( Sample ) and using the indicated kit, ratios around 2.0 can be reached, but more commonly the ratios are within 1.7-1.9; this poses little to no issues in most downstream NGS applications.

steps.title: Clean and repeat the read at...
steps.instruction: Clean and repeat the read at least once for consistency check. Some variability in readings is relatively common.

steps.title: Pipette a drop (2 μL recommended...
steps.instruction: Pipette a drop (2 μL recommended volume) of the sample into the holder in the reader. No dilution is needed if using similar biomass as indicated for the reference sample in this protocol ( Sample )

steps.title: Prepare a 0
steps.instruction: Prepare a 0.8% agarose gel stained with Invitrogen SYBR Safe DNA Gel Stain Invitrogen - Thermo Fisher , following the manufacturer instructions.

steps.title: Run an electrophoresis at 100V for 30 min
steps.instruction: Run an electrophoresis at 100V for 30 min.

steps.title: Place the gel in an electrophoresis...
steps.instruction: Place the gel in an electrophoresis tank filled with an appropriate electrophoresis buffer (e.g. 10X TBE Buffer Thermo Fisher Scientific , diluted to 1x) and load the samples with an appropriate loading dye (e.g., 6X DNA Loading Dye Thermo Fisher Scientific , diluted to 1x). In the first slot add the ladder (e.g., GeneRuler 1 kb Plus DNA Ladder Thermo Fisher Catalog #SM1331 ).

steps.title: Take your gel to a gel...
steps.instruction: Take your gel to a gel documentation platform and check the bands pattern. Ideally, integer DNA fragments should be on the top of the gel, above the 10,000 bp ladder band.

steps.title: Aliquot the samples (≈15 µl) for quality control checks
steps.instruction: Aliquot the samples (≈15 µl) for quality control checks.

steps.title: Store DNA at -80°C or keep...
steps.instruction: Store DNA at -80°C or keep it at 4°C if preservation for a short period is intended before further steps.
